title: Winner Summary
description: Canonical contract for `results/{pair_id}/winner_summary.json`. Produced by Econ Evan at tournament completion; consumed by AppDev Ace's Strategy page (probability engine panel, position adjustment panel, instructional trigger cards, execution panel) and by Research Ray for narrative cross-reference. Resolves ECON-H5 (producer mandate) and APP-WS1 (consumer pre-render contract) in a single artifact per META-CF. Companion to `tournament_winner.json` (META-TWJ) — winner_summary is the render-ready strategy descriptor; tournament_winner is the winner-vs-benchmark delta record.

pair_id.description: Canonical pair identifier (snake_case, e.g. `hy_ig_v2_spy`). Matches the `results/{pair_id}/` directory name and the landing-card key.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time at which the tournament winner was finalized and this file was written. Used for regression diff and rerun detection.

signal_column.description: **Canonical answer to APP-WS1**: the EXACT parquet column name in `results/{pair_id}/signals_{date}.parquet` that Ace's consumer code (`pd.read_parquet(...)[signal_column]`) must read to resolve the winning strategy's underlying signal. Example: `hmm_2state_prob_stress`. MUST NOT be a display name and MUST NOT be the tournament signal_code — use `signal_code` for that purpose. Producers that rename the parquet column MUST update this field in the same commit.

signal_code.description: Tournament signal identifier (e.g. `S6_hmm_stress`, `S3_ROC`). The taxonomy label used in tournament CSVs and registries. Distinct from `signal_column`: a single signal_code may map to different parquet column names across pairs if the underlying signal is re-parameterized.

target_symbol.description: Target asset ticker (e.g. `SPY`, `AGG`, `TLT`). Matches the Analysis Brief Section 3 target and the pair's benchmark series. Ace reads this to label KPI cards and trigger cards; never hardcode a fallback.

threshold_value.description: Numeric threshold used by the winning strategy rule. Units depend on `signal_column` semantics (probability in [0,1] for HMM signals; z-score units for z-score signals; etc.). Pair with `threshold_rule` to form the complete decision rule.

threshold_rule.description: Comparison operator applied to `signal_column` vs `threshold_value`. `gt`/`lt`/`gte`/`lte` are static comparisons (entry = signal satisfies relation at t); `crosses_up`/`crosses_down` are event triggers (entry = signal crossed the threshold in direction between t-1 and t).

strategy_family.description: Position-sizing family. `P1_long_cash` = binary long/cash; `P2_signal_strength` = continuous scaling by signal magnitude; `P3_long_short` = symmetric long/short. Ace's Position Adjustment panel (APP-SE2) branches on this value.

direction.description: Empirical relationship between signal and target observed in the tournament winner. `procyclical` = higher signal coincides with higher target returns; `countercyclical` = higher signal coincides with lower target returns (typical of stress/risk signals); `mixed` = sign depends on regime or threshold. Mirrors `interpretation_metadata.json.observed_direction`.

oos_sharpe.description: Out-of-sample annualized Sharpe ratio of the winning strategy. Risk-free assumption follows the pair's Analysis Brief target-class convention (SOFR for equities/fixed income).

oos_ann_return.description: Out-of-sample annualized return as a ratio (e.g. 0.113 = 11.3%). Units: ratio (decimal). Computed as geometric annualization of OOS equity curve.

oos_max_drawdown.description: Out-of-sample maximum drawdown as a ratio (negative, e.g. -0.102 = -10.2%). MUST be negative or zero; positive values are a producer-side bug.

oos_n_trades.description: Count of position changes in the OOS window. A position change is any non-zero exposure delta crossing the threshold rule.

oos_period_start.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 start date of the OOS evaluation window (YYYY-MM-DD).

oos_period_end.description: Inclusive ISO 8601 end date of the OOS evaluation window (YYYY-MM-DD).

bh_sharpe.description: Buy-and-hold benchmark Sharpe over the same OOS window. Recommended for KPI-card delta rendering; if omitted, Ace falls back to `tournament_winner.json.benchmark.oos_sharpe`.

bh_ann_return.description: Buy-and-hold benchmark annualized return over the same OOS window (ratio, e.g. 0.147 = 14.7%).

annual_turnover.description: Annualized trade frequency (number of position changes per year). Drives transaction-cost estimation and liquidity stress warnings.

cost_assumption_bps.description: Per-trade transaction cost assumption applied during backtest, in basis points. Target-class default: 5 bps for equities/ETFs (see ECON-T2).

notes.description: Freeform producer notes (caveats, sample-period warnings, known limitations). Rendered verbatim by Ace in the Strategy-page 'Important Caveats' section when present.
